highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.119 Released
highlights.summary: Settings configured via `/config` (like theme, editor mode, and verbose output) now persist to `~/.claude/settings.json` and respect project/local/policy override rules. A new `prUrlTemplate` setting lets you point the PR badge footer to a custom code-review URL instead of GitHub. This makes Claude Code more portable and team-friendly across different workflows.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.118 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds vim visual mode (`v`) and visual-line mode (`V`) with full selection and operator support, great news for keyboard-driven developers. The `/cost` and `/stats` commands have been merged into a single `/usage` command with tabbed views, reducing clutter. You can also now create and switch between named custom themes directly from `/theme`.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.116 Released
highlights.summary: Resuming large sessions (40MB+) is now up to 67% faster, making it much more practical to pick up long-running projects where you left off. MCP startup is also quicker when multiple stdio servers are configured, as resource template listing is now deferred until first `@`-mention. These performance improvements reduce friction for power users with complex setups.

highlights.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
highlights.summary: Anthropic published an engineering postmortem addressing community reports of degraded Claude Code output quality around April 23. This is an important read if you've noticed unexpected behavior or regressions in recent sessions. The postmortem likely includes root cause analysis and steps taken to restore reliability.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest iteration of their most capable model tier. Developers using Claude Code can expect this model to be available for selection, potentially improving the quality of complex code generation and reasoning tasks. Check the announcement for details on capability improvements and pricing.

highlights.title: How to Make Claude Code Your AI Engineering Team
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial explores strategies for using Claude Code as a full AI engineering team, covering multi-agent workflows and task delegation patterns. It's well-suited for developers who want to move beyond single prompts and start orchestrating Claude for larger, real-world projects. Watch this to learn how to scale your use of Claude Code beyond basic code generation.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: A compact, focused tutorial designed to get developers productive with Claude Code quickly, covering the most important features and workflows in under half an hour. This is ideal if you want a structured overview without committing to a longer course. By the end, you should be comfortable with core commands, file editing, and basic agent interactions.

tips_of_the_day: Run `/config` to adjust your Claude Code settings, then check `~/.claude/settings.json` to confirm they persisted — this file can be committed to a shared dotfiles repo so your preferences travel with you across machines.

If a long session feels slow to resume, upgrade to v2.1.116 or later; sessions over 40MB now resume up to 67% faster, so don't hesitate to keep detailed, long-running project sessions instead of starting fresh.
Try switching your active model to Claude Opus 4.7 via `/model` when tackling complex architectural decisions or tricky bugs — save faster models for routine edits to manage costs efficiently.
